# UC-21.8.12 · FreePBX Asterisk PJSIP Endpoint Registration Flapping

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Availability, Fault &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch phone extension registration flaps. We help you catch flaky endpoints before calls drop in bulk.*

---

## Description

PJSIP registrations that oscillate between reachable and unreachable degrade both inbound DIDs and emergency calling. Mining Asterisk full logs for unreachable transitions and removed contacts exposes flaky trunks or UDP timeout misconfigurations before helpdesk volume spikes.

## Value

Improves voice availability by catching registration flaps early—often fixable with qualify frequency, TLS, or firewall UDP timeout tuning.

## Implementation

Forward `/var/log/asterisk/full` with `sourcetype=asterisk:full` (props can also split `pjsip` facility if you use structured logging). Extract `endpoint` or `AOR` identifiers. Alert when event rate per endpoint crosses threshold in 10 minutes. Enrich with edge firewall logs if available.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Time sync NTP on Asterisk host and Splunk indexer.
- `TIME_FORMAT` in props if timestamps are non-standard.

### Step 1 — Tune rex for your distro’s log format.

### Step 2 — Exclude known maintenance windows.

### Step 3 — Validate
Compare calculated buckets with the MES shift report or historian (for example PI or Proficy) for the same line and period. Confirm planned versus run time matches the routing master used by production.

### Step 4 — Document carrier escalation when trunk-side.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip (sourcetype=asterisk:full OR sourcetype=asterisk:pjsip)
| search "Endpoint" "Unreachable" OR "Removed contact" OR "REGISTER"
| rex field=_raw "Endpoint\s+(?<endpoint>\S+)"
| bin _time span=10m
| stats count as state_events, dc(_raw) as unique_msgs by _time, host, endpoint
| where state_events > 15
```

## Visualization

Timeline (state_events by endpoint), Table (worst endpoints), Choropleth (site) if geo-tagged.

## Known False Positives

Registration flaps during phone reprovisioning, TLS profile updates, or remote-worker VPN instability that the UC admin correlates to client upgrades.

## References

- [Asterisk Documentation — PJSIP configuration](https://docs.asterisk.org/Configuration/Channel-Drivers/SIP/Configuring-res_pjsip/)
- [FreePBX Wiki — Asterisk Logs](https://wiki.freepbx.org/display/PPS/Asterisk+Logs)
